intro: The clearest split is that Daily lists entry pricing at $10 versus $399.00/mo for Stream. Daily is Real-time video and audio API infrastructure for embedding calls in web and mobile applications; Stream is Activity feed and chat API infrastructure with scalable feed aggregation, reactions, and moderation. The data points Daily at chat applications and live collaboration features, and Stream at chat applications and live collaboration features. Pricing: Daily is subscription from $10; Stream is subscription from $399.00/mo. Lock-in is medium for Daily and medium for Stream, with DX at 5/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 4/5. On listed strengths, Daily emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features, and Stream emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: neither fits static projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: Daily is for chat applications; Stream is for chat applications; decide based on your entry-price budget.
verdict_a: Choose Daily if an entry price of $10 fits your budget and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.
verdict_b: Choose Stream if an entry price of $399.00/mo fits your budget and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.

faq.a: Listed entry pricing is $10 for Daily and $399.00/mo for Stream. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Daily to Stream?
faq.a: Daily is tagged with medium lock-in (video/audio sdk platform). Stream is tagged with medium lock-in (feed/chat api, data exportable). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Daily or Stream?
faq.a: Both are rated 5/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is Stream a good alternative to Daily?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches chat applications, since both list the same fit. Stream is flagged as not for static projects, so confirm your context does not fall there.
